spec_match.rationale: Page runs script and renders dynamic UI, but the visible API surface (translate() only partially mirrors what the feature summary calls out — the headline behaviour isn't unambiguously demonstrated.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (3 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (installed, migration, origin, user).

edge_cases.rationale: No visible edge case / fallback / error handling — only the happy path.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus link is present but the page has no dedicated `references` section — the self-heal contract is fragile.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 54 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Tighten the link between the demo and the spec's headline behaviour
openQuestions.detail: Confirm the on-screen interaction maps to a concrete API call from the feature summary on chromestatus: "When a user installs a Progressive Web App (PWA), its identity and security context are tightly bound to its web origin, for example, `app.example.com`. This presents a significant challenge for devel". If the call is being shimmed or simulated, surface that fact in the page and add a real invocation behind a feature-detect.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Add explicit edge case coverage
openQuestions.detail: Pick one failure mode the spec or chromestatus motivation calls out (unsupported browser, denied permission, empty input, etc.) and surface it as a distinct demo state alongside the happy path.

openQuestions.title: Wrap references in a labelled `references` section
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, the chromestatus link is the self-heal anchor and should sit inside a clearly-labelled `references` section so the recovery script can find it reliably.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("When a user installs a Progressive Web App (PWA), its identity and security context are tightly bound to its web origin, for example, `app.example.com`. This presents a significant challenge for developers who need to change their PWA's ori") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://github.com/WICG/manifest-incubations/pull/136 to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~1411 chars of script across 3 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 2 pass / 3 partial / 1 fail across the six rubric criteria.
